commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an internal cron setup-timeout callback, wires gateway cron to request a safe gateway restart for that timeout class, and adds focused cron/gateway regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +59, Tests +90. Total +149 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main has a pre-runner setup-timeout path and no gateway restart callback for it. I did not run a live current-main repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer review of the automatic restart policy plus contributor-supplied PR-head real behavior proof, not a narrow repair that automation can safely perform.

target.title: fix: restart gateway after isolated cron setup timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- classify isolated cron setup timeouts as a gateway-health failure class
- notify the gateway cron service when setup stalls before the isolated runner starts
- request a safe gateway restart through the existing restart coordinator so affected recurring jobs can recover centrally
- add cron-service and gateway regression coverage for the restart path

## Root Cause
Cron jobs that use isolated agent turns can fail before the runner starts with `cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start`. Today that error is recorded and alerted, but it is not treated as a platform-level gateway/isolated-runner health failure. Individual cron workflows can self-repair around it, but the shared failure mode keeps recurring across unrelated jobs.

## Impact
When this failure class occurs, OpenClaw now asks the existing safe-restart coordinator to restart only after the normal preflight drain says it is safe. This should reduce repeated user-visible cron failure cards without interrupting active work.

## Real behavior proof
Observed on a real macOS OpenClaw setup before the fix path: OpenClaw status could not reach the gateway, recurring cron cards repeatedly reported `cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start`, and the gateway node process was hot/stale at about 109% CPU with roughly 2.8GB RSS. A process sample showed the gateway stuck in V8 timer/microtask/object-copying/GC paths while isolated setup continued to fail before runner start.

Applied the operational equivalent of this PR's recovery path by using OpenClaw's normal update/restart flow, which kills the stale gateway and lets LaunchAgent bring it back. After restart, live checks showed:

- `openclaw --version`: `OpenClaw 2026.5.28 (e932160)`
- `openclaw gateway status`: LaunchAgent loaded, runtime running, connectivity probe OK, gateway version `2026.5.28`, admin-capable, listening on `127.0.0.1:18789`
- `openclaw status --json`: `gateway.reachable=true`, `gateway.connectLatencyMs=45`, `tasks.byStatus.queued=0`, and no queued system events
- gateway process `ps`: pid `93467`, around `1.0%` CPU, `3.2%` memory, RSS `534560` KB

That recovered the live setup without suppressing normal OpenClaw work. A YC YouTube channel ingest is currently able to run through the restored gateway instead of the previous stale-gateway/setup-timeout loop.

## Testing
- `pnpm test src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `git diff --check`

Note: `pnpm check:changed` was also attempted locally before this PR, but the local Blacksmith Testbox wrapper failed its own `--version/--help` sanity check before project checks could run.
target.author: ghitafilali
